title: v3 outage is rendered as $0 when v2 data exists
reasoning: `hasSnapshotError` represents the v3 PoolDailySnapshot rollup being unavailable or truncated, while `hasBrokerSnapshotError` covers the independent v2 Broker rollup. The headline only returns `N/A` for a v3 snapshot error when both v3 and v2 point arrays are empty. If the v3 snapshot query fails with no surviving v3 points but the v2 Broker query succeeds, `v3Partial` remains false and the headline renders `$0.00 v3 · $X v2`, which misrepresents unknown v3 volume as a confirmed zero. The component already treats the symmetric v2 failure as unavailable (`— v2`), so v3 should get the same unavailable treatment when its own data source failed and no v3 data survived.
recommendation: Split v3 availability from total chart availability. When `hasSnapshotError && v3Points.length === 0`, render the v3 cell as `—` unless both version series are empty and the intended full-card state is `N/A`. Keep the partial-data marker so users still see the degraded state.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current snapshot-error test only covers the case where no data survived at all, and the broker-error test only covers the v2 outage path. There is no test for `hasSnapshotError: true` with surviving v2 Broker rows and empty v3 rows.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a render test with `hasSnapshotError: true`, `networkData` containing only `brokerSnapshotsAllDaily`, and no v3 snapshots. Assert the headline contains `—` next to the v3 badge, the v2 dollar total is still shown, and `$0.00` is not rendered next to the v3 badge.
minimumFixScope: Update `computeHeadline` to represent v3 unavailability separately from v3 zero volume, and add the focused render regression test in `volume-over-time-chart.test.tsx`.

history.note: VolumeOverTimeChart now renders v3 unavailable when v3 snapshots fail but v2 broker data survives, with focused headline regression.
